prerequisites: A Docker Hub account username to query. Provide it as the required `docker_username` config field. The connector reads publicly available Docker Hub data (repositories, tags, and metadata) for this account.
Public Docker Hub repositories under that username, since this connector does not supply secret credentials and reads only data that is accessible without authentication.
Awareness of Docker Hub rate limits: unauthenticated requests are limited to 100 pulls per 6 hours per IPv4 address or IPv6 /64 subnet, so high-volume syncs may be throttled.

authMethods.name: Anonymous (public access via username)
authMethods.summary: This connector defines no secret fields. It authenticates with nothing beyond the public `docker_username` and reads only Docker Hub data that is accessible without credentials. Docker Hub exposes public repository, tag, and account metadata anonymously, which is what this connector consumes. Note that anonymous access is subject to Docker Hub's unauthenticated rate limit of 100 pulls per 6 hours per IP.

setupSteps.title: Identify the Docker Hub username
setupSteps.body: Decide which Docker Hub account you want to extract from. This is the account whose public namespace contains the repositories and tags you want to sync. The value is the username portion of a Docker Hub URL such as hub.docker.com/u/<docker_username>.

setupSteps.title: Set the docker_username config field
setupSteps.body: Enter the username into the required `docker_username` field. This is the only configuration field for this connector and it is not a secret. The connector uses it to scope all Docker Hub API requests to that account's public repositories and tags.

setupSteps.title: Verify the data is public
setupSteps.body: Because this connector provides no secret credentials, it can only read repositories that are publicly visible. Confirm the target repositories under the username are public; private repositories will not be readable without authentication, which this connector does not perform.

setupSteps.title: Optional: reduce rate-limit throttling
setupSteps.body: If you hit Docker Hub's unauthenticated pull rate limit (100 per 6 hours per IPv4 address or IPv6 /64 subnet), run the sync from an IP with available quota or spread syncs over time. Higher limits on Docker Hub require an authenticated account (for example a Personal account gets 200 pulls per 6 hours, and Pro, Team, and Business accounts are unlimited), but this connector does not accept a token, so it cannot raise its own limit.

sources.title: Docker Hub usage and rate limits
